prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging populations and improved detection

incidence.source: European VTE cohort syntheses / ESC context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Pre-test probability (Wells/Geneva), D-dimer, confirmation with CTPA; risk stratification per ESC guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; strong inpatient prophylaxis programs in many countries
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with prompt diagnosis and standardized anticoagulation pathways

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Broad access to D-dimer/CTPA and risk stratification tools in emergency care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: DOAC-first strategies common for many patients; LMWH in cancer/pregnancy contexts; thrombolysis/thrombectomy for high-risk PE in specialist centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising burden with aging and care variability across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: ESC guideline-driven pathways, high diagnostic access, and widespread anticoagulant availability
